purpose: Executable closure contracts for every readiness item that is not yet shipped or implemented because it requires external partner evidence, package publication, public benchmark data, or live certification.
definition_of_done: Every open readiness requirement has exactly one work order.
Every work order separates local build/proof from external evidence.
Every work order lists files, commands, evidence required, public-copy rule, and failure modes.
No work order may convert an external/package/benchmark/certification gate to shipped without real evidence.

workorders.public_copy_rule: Say the .kolm spec is published and foundation-ready; do not claim CNCF, Linux Foundation, standards-body, Hugging Face, Ollama, or llama.cpp adoption until accepted by the external party.
external_actions: Create a neutral governance proposal with spec versioning, compatibility policy, verifier API, conformance test plan, and trademark rules.
Submit to a neutral venue or create a public foundation steering process.
Get written acceptance, incubation, or public governance commitment from the external venue.
evidence_required: accepted proposal or signed steering minutes
failure_modes: Public copy says standard-backed before a neutral venue accepts it.
Spec changes without compatibility policy.
Verifier/conformance suite is not usable outside the hosted Kolm app.

workorders.public_copy_rule: Say Kolm provides runtime adoption packets and conformance fixtures; do not claim native support in Hugging Face, Ollama, llama.cpp, ONNX/GGUF tooling, or hardware vendor stacks until merged or publicly released by those projects.
external_actions: Prepare minimal .kolm loader examples for each target runtime.
Open upstream issues or PRs with conformance fixtures and standalone verifier docs.
Capture merged PR, release note, partner blog, or signed integration statement.
evidence_required: upstream merged PR or released package
partner permission to name the integration
failure_modes: A demo adapter is treated as native adoption.
Runtime integration lacks signature verification.
Public matrix implies support for targets that only have local packets.

workorders.public_copy_rule: Say browser runtime/package work is locally prepared; do not advertise npm-installable runtime-wasm until a signed release artifact or registry URL exists.
external_actions: Cut a signed release archive containing browser/WASM runtime artifacts.
Publish npm package or release URL and lock checksum into package readiness manifest.
Run browser smoke on a clean install and attach result to the release.
evidence_required: signed release artifact or npm registry URL
failure_modes: Docs show `npm install` before publication.
WASM artifact has no signature or checksum.
Browser runtime passes local imports but fails clean package install.

workorders.public_copy_rule: Say iOS, Android, and React Native SDK sources exist locally; do not claim App Store, CocoaPods, SwiftPM, Maven Central, or npm package availability until release evidence exists.
external_actions: Publish Swift package tag and CocoaPods spec if used.
Publish Kotlin artifact to Maven or provide signed GitHub release.
Publish React Native package to npm with native iOS/Android smoke evidence.
evidence_required: Swift package tag or registry URL
npm package URL for React Native
failure_modes: Mobile docs claim install availability while package channels are pending.
Native bridge package lacks platform sample smoke.
Release artifacts have no checksum or signed provenance.

workorders.public_copy_rule: Say local benchmark harnesses and fixture evidence exist; do not claim public leaderboard superiority or production benchmark numbers until reproducible public runs are published.
external_actions: Freeze benchmark dataset versions, hardware targets, model/provider versions, pricing assumptions, and exact commands.
Run public reproducible benchmark jobs and publish raw logs, manifests, receipts, and signed result bundle.
Update public benchmark pages only after raw data is linked.
failure_modes: Synthetic fixture results are marketed as public benchmark evidence.
Competitor comparisons use stale prices or unpinned model versions.
Benchmark page links summaries without raw reproducible output.

workorders.public_copy_rule: Say SDK source packages and local dry-run checks exist; do not claim public package-manager availability until the registry or signed-release URL exists for each SDK.
external_actions: Publish or sign each SDK package.
Record registry URL, version, checksum, provenance, and clean-install log.
Update SDK docs from the release manifest, not hand-written install copy.
evidence_required: registry URL or signed release URL per SDK
failure_modes: SDK matrix says package exists before it is published.
Docs drift from actual package manifest names.
Language SDK release lacks clean-install smoke.

workorders.public_copy_rule: Say evidence packets, control mappings, and templates are ready; do not claim SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA BAA, GDPR DPA, FedRAMP, or formal SLSA status until signed evidence exists.
external_actions: Engage auditor/counsel for SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA BAA, GDPR DPA, FedRAMP boundary, and SLSA provenance review as applicable.
Attach signed reports, countersigned agreements, or formal authorization letters.
Update readiness statuses only from signed evidence, not from internal readiness.
evidence_required: signed SOC 2 or auditor report when claimed
signed SLSA/SBOM provenance evidence when claimed
failure_modes: Security page claims certification because controls are implemented locally.
Compliance packet contains placeholders or unsigned templates.
Product copy collapses HIPAA-ready workflow into HIPAA-certified status.

workorders.public_copy_rule: Say install scripts and package manifests exist; do not publish `brew install`, `apt install`, `winget install`, or curl-pipe install as generally available until release artifacts and checksums are real.
external_actions: Cut signed CLI release archives for supported OS/architectures.
Replace placeholder checksums in Homebrew, apt, winget, and install scripts.
Submit package-manager PRs or publish signed release artifacts with clean install logs.
failure_modes: Public install copy points at package managers before release artifacts exist.
Installer downloads unsigned binaries.
Generated package manifest carries placeholder SHA256 values.
